mcqs.question: What is thrashing in an operating system?

options.text: A sudden increase in CPU utilization
A state where the system spends more time paging than executing
A technique to improve memory allocation
A method to reduce context switching

mcqs.explanation: Thrashing occurs when the system is overwhelmed by page faults, leading to excessive disk I/O and negligible progress in process execution.

mcqs.question: Which of the following causes thrashing?

options.text: High CPU utilization
Insufficient memory for active processes
Frequent context switching
Low degree of multiprogramming

mcqs.explanation: Thrashing is primarily caused by insufficient memory to hold the working sets of all active processes, leading to excessive paging.

mcqs.question: What is the working set of a process?

options.text: The set of pages a process will never use
The set of pages a process is currently using
The set of pages stored in disk
The set of pages allocated by the OS

mcqs.explanation: The working set is the collection of pages a process actively uses within a specific time frame, reflecting its locality of reference.

mcqs.question: How can thrashing be mitigated?

options.text: By increasing the degree of multiprogramming
By reducing the number of processes in memory
By using a larger page size
By implementing the working set model

mcqs.explanation: The working set model ensures processes have enough memory for their active pages, preventing excessive page faults.

mcqs.question: What happens during thrashing?

options.text: CPU utilization approaches 100%
Disk I/O becomes the bottleneck
Processes complete execution faster
Memory fragmentation decreases

mcqs.explanation: During thrashing, the system is busy swapping pages, causing disk I/O to dominate and CPU utilization to drop sharply.
